name: United Arab Emirates
summary: Day 75 of Hormuz blockade. **A10 NEWSWEEK + WSJ + 2 WESTERN OFFICIALS + 2 IRANIAN OFFICIALS REPORTED MAY 12-13: SAUDI ARABIA LAUNCHED SECRET STRIKES AGAINST IRAN IN LATE MARCH ('tit-for-tat retaliation when Saudi was hit') ON TOP OF YESTERDAY'S UAE-LAVAN WSJ DISCLOSURE (April 8 Mirage 2000-9 attack). Newsweek also reports Iran has fired >2,800 missiles/drones at UAE territory CUMULATIVELY — MORE THAN AT ANY OTHER COUNTRY — Iran's prior 'punishment of US-aligned host' framing against UAE is now confirmed retaliation against a fully kinetic third combatant. ONE OF THE UAE LAVAN-RESPONSE STRIKES IS REPORTED AS 'COORDINATED WITH ISRAEL.' Saudi has not publicly acknowledged late-March strikes; Iran has not commented; Al Hadath (Saudi) ran editorial distance language on UAE story but has not engaged the Saudi-strikes claim. The Gulf-alignment graph now requires Saudi to move from 'OPEC+/balancing/underwriter' toward partial kinetic participant — STRUCTURAL RE-ENCODING OF ENTIRE GULF ROW.** **Modi arrives in UAE May 15 and in this new context his Gulf-cousin visit lands in a more politically loaded environment than even yesterday's reading anticipated.** Iran FM Araghchi will land in New Delhi for BRICS May 13-14 (sees Modi May 14 same day Trump in Beijing) — see iran node. **Khaleej Times ran Modi UAE-visit piece factually focused on energy security with ZERO editorial framing of UAE's recently-disclosed combatant role; The National (Abu Dhabi) ran Trump-Beijing trip 'overshadowed by Iran war' but again did NOT engage Lavan disclosure or new Saudi-strikes claim — same kid-gloves silence as yesterday. UAE state-aligned media treating both stories as something to wait out.** No Indian outlet has editorialised against Modi visiting UAE knowing UAE struck Iran — diaspora-base in UAE (~3.5M Indians) makes anti-UAE framing politically unworkable. Iran-side prior UAE-targeted strikes are the implicit operational response (CONFIRMED ACTION); explicit Iranian re-attribution NOT made publicly. PRIOR CONTEXT (May 12): **B6 WSJ REVELATION MAY 11 (MAJOR): UAE AIR FORCE SECRETLY CARRIED OUT AIRSTRIKES INSIDE IRAN DURING THE WAR, INCLUDING AN APRIL 8 STRIKE ON THE LAVAN ISLAND OIL REFINERY (Persian Gulf, 55,000 bpd capacity, knocked offline for months) USING MIRAGE 2000-9 AIRCRAFT.** UAE has not publicly acknowledged. Washington reportedly 'positively received' Abu Dhabi's participation. **REFRAMES ENTIRE REGIONAL ALIGNMENT PICTURE: UAE moves from 'passive US host with hawkish air defense' to THE ONLY THIRD COUNTRY TO JOIN US-ISRAEL KINETIC OPERATIONS AGAINST IRAN.** Iran's prior March/April UAE-targeted missile and drone strikes — previously framed as Iran 'targeting US-aligned Gulf hosts' — are now retroactively contextualized as DIRECT RETALIATION for confirmed covert UAE participation in offensive ops on Iran. Saudi Arabia, by contrast, denied via Al-Hadath May 8 that it allowed offensive airspace use — visibly distancing. **UAE silent on WSJ exposure; The National (UAE state-aligned) conspicuously absent from leading the Lavan story — ran a piece headlined 'Trump heads to Beijing... overshadowed by Iran war' but did NOT amplify WSJ's revelation. UAE state-aligned media treating WSJ scoop as something to wait out, not engage. Iran silent on WSJ exposure (the operational response IS the prior UAE-targeted strikes, retroactively reframed); Khaleej Times continued straight reporting on UAE-Iran kinetic events but did not editorialize.** Gulf-state alignment fracture is now visible: UAE (only third combatant, covertly), Saudi (deniable-operational + public-pro-mediation, distancing from UAE military track), Qatar (mediating with IRGC contacts), Bahrain (US-aligned counter-IRGC), Kuwait (denied airspace), Oman (silent). **Iran on May 8 retaliated against UAE with 2 ballistic + 3 drones (3 wounded confirmed) — now retroactively readable as response to UAE's role.** **Modi 5-nation tour May 15-20 (UAE-Netherlands-Sweden-Norway-Italy) — India is engaging with only third combatant nation simultaneously with diversification framing; UAE leg materially complicated by Lavan revelation.** UAE airspace partial closure post-Fujairah ended on/around May 11 (PRE-confirmed timeline). The Saudi-UAE structural overhang persists.

active_triggers.condition: UAE enters war as combatant (not just defensive)
UAE adds 500K-1M bpd of supply within 6 months of OPEC exit (May 1) confirming Atlantic Council/Derentz medium-term-bearish thesis
Saudi Arabia issues formal public response to UAE OPEC exit within 14 days (by 2026-05-12)

recent_signals.headline: A10 NEWSWEEK + WSJ + 2 Western + 2 Iranian officials May 12-13: SAUDI ARABIA LAUNCHED SECRET STRIKES AGAINST IRAN IN LATE MARCH ('tit-for-tat retaliation when Saudi was hit') ON TOP OF YESTERDAY'S UAE-LAVAN WSJ DISCLOSURE (April 8 Mirage 2000-9). Newsweek separately reports Iran has fired >2,800 missiles/drones at UAE territory cumulatively — MORE THAN AT ANY OTHER COUNTRY — Iran's prior 'punishment of US-aligned host' framing now confirmed retaliation against fully kinetic third combatant. One of the UAE Lavan-response strikes reported as COORDINATED WITH ISRAEL. Saudi has not publicly acknowledged; Iran silent; Al Hadath (Saudi) editorial distance on UAE story but silent on Saudi-strikes. **B5 PM MODI 5-NATION TOUR CONFIRMED MAY 15-20 (UAE first, then Netherlands/Sweden/Norway/Italy); explicit UAE focus on 'energy security for India amid US-Iran war' + Comprehensive Strategic Partnership (Khaleej Times, India TV, The Print, US News).** Modi will land in UAE May 15 (now confirmed covert combatant) in a more politically loaded environment than even yesterday's reading anticipated, while Iran FM Araghchi is in Delhi for BRICS May 13-14 — Modi's most overt strategic-autonomy choreography of cycle. No Indian outlet has editorialised against Modi visiting UAE knowing UAE struck Iran — diaspora-base ~3.5M Indians in UAE makes anti-UAE framing politically unworkable. The National (Abu Dhabi) ran Trump-Beijing 'overshadowed by Iran war' piece but did NOT engage Lavan or Saudi-strikes; Khaleej Times ran Modi visit factually with zero editorial framing of UAE's covert combatant role — UAE state-aligned media treating both stories as something to wait out, same kid-gloves silence as yesterday.
sources: Newsweek 'Iran War Update: Saudi Arabia and UAE Involved Militarily' (primary)
WSJ primary (cited by Newsweek + Times of Israel + Middle East Eye)
Middle East Eye 'Report says UAE carried out secret strikes' (regional)
India TV 'PM Modi five-nation visit May 15-20'
Khaleej Times 'India's Modi to begin five-nation tour' — UAE state
The Print 'Modi 5-nation tour energy emerging tech'
US News 'India's Modi to Begin Five-Nation Tour'
The National (Abu Dhabi) — UAE state-aligned (conspicuous non-engagement on Lavan + Saudi-strikes)
Al-Hadath (Saudi-state, editorial distance, no Saudi-strikes engagement)
recent_signals.verification: Saudi strikes Iran late March = REPORTED (Newsweek+WSJ single primary cluster; Saudi silent; Iran silent) — DO NOT ELEVATE; UAE Modi 5-nation tour confirmed May 15-20 = CONFIRMED multi-source government schedule (Khaleej Times state primary + India TV + The Print + US News); >2,800 Iran missiles/drones at UAE cumulative = REPORTED (Newsweek single relay); UAE state-aligned media non-engagement of Lavan + Saudi-strikes = CONFIRMED via The National conspicuous absence from leading either story.

recent_signals.headline: WSJ MAY 11 (MAJOR RETROACTIVE REVELATION): UAE AIR FORCE SECRETLY STRUCK IRAN'S LAVAN ISLAND OIL REFINERY APRIL 8 USING MIRAGE 2000-9 (55,000 bpd capacity offline months) — UAE moves from passive US host with hawkish air defense to THE ONLY THIRD COUNTRY TO JOIN US-ISRAEL KINETIC OPERATIONS AGAINST IRAN. 'Washington reportedly positively received Abu Dhabi's participation.' UAE has not publicly acknowledged. Iran silent on WSJ exposure (operational response IS prior UAE-targeted strikes retroactively reframed). REFRAMES regional alignment: Iran's March/April UAE-targeted missile/drone strikes were direct retaliation for confirmed covert UAE offensive ops, not just 'targeting US-aligned Gulf hosts.' Saudi visibly distancing (denied via Al-Hadath May 8 airspace use for offensive). The National (UAE state-aligned) conspicuously absent from leading WSJ story — UAE state-aligned media treating WSJ scoop as something to wait out. Modi 5-nation tour May 15-20 (UAE first leg) materially complicated by Lavan revelation — India engaging with only third combatant nation simultaneously with diversification framing. UAE airspace partial closure post-Fujairah ended ~May 11.
sources: The National UAE (notable conspicuous absence from leading the story)
recent_signals.verification: UAE Lavan April 8 strike via Mirage 2000-9 = REPORTED (WSJ primary, single-source on full operational details; UAE has not publicly acknowledged); Saudi denial of offensive airspace use = CONFIRMED DIPLOMATIC SIGNAL via Al-Hadath; UAE state-aligned media non-engagement = CONFIRMED via The National's conspicuous absence from leading the story; Iran-side prior UAE-targeted strikes are the implicit operational response (CONFIRMED ACTION); explicit Iranian re-attribution NOT made publicly

recent_signals.headline: UAE AIR-DEFENSE ENGAGEMENT MAY 8 SCALE REFINED (UPDATED — yesterday's brief understated): 2 BALLISTIC MISSILES + 3 DRONES LAUNCHED BY IRAN TOWARD UAE; 3 PEOPLE WOUNDED CONFIRMED PER UAE DEFENSE MINISTRY; not clear whether all incoming weapons were intercepted. Strike followed CENTCOM's same-day announcement of thwarting attacks on three US Navy ships in Strait of Hormuz. SECOND major UAE-targeted Iranian attack of cycle (first: May 4 Fujairah refinery strike). UAE now demonstrably on IRGC operational targeting list. Gulf bifurcation now operationally specific: UAE (Iranian targets / hawkish), Bahrain (US-aligned counter-IRGC, 41 arrests May 9), Qatar (mediating via IRGC contacts), Saudi (deniable-operational + public-pro-mediation), Oman (silent), Kuwait (denied airspace). The Saudi-UAE structural overhang persists.
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED ACTION (UAE Defense Ministry primary; 3 wounded confirmed; 2 ballistic + 3 drones; multi-source AP wire); Iran has not officially claimed but Tasnim/Iranian state media did not deny

recent_signals.headline: UAE AIR-DEFENSE BREAKDOWN REFINED MAY 4 STRIKE: 12 BALLISTIC + 3 CRUISE + 4 DRONES = 19 VEHICLES; 18 INTERCEPTED (~95% EFFECTIVENESS); UAE FM (NOT RULER) SENT TO MBS JEDDAH SUMMIT — DIPLOMATIC DISTANCE SIGNAL; PM MODI PERSONALLY CONDEMNED FUJAIRAH ATTACK ('UNACCEPTABLE')
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED UAE primary on intercept breakdown; Modi statement = CONFIRMED primary PMO event (ANI + Tribune + BT + MEA spokesperson Jaiswal)

recent_signals.headline: FUJAIRAH OIL INDUSTRY ZONE STRUCK BY IRANIAN DRONE MAY 4 — FIRST UAE ATTACK SINCE APR 8 CEASEFIRE; 3 INDIAN NATIONALS INJURED; 4 CRUISE MISSILES INTERCEPTED OR FELL INTO SEA; UAE AIRSPACE PARTIAL CLOSURE MAY 4-11
UAE OPEC EXIT EFFECTIVE MAY 1 — DAY 2-3 OF POST-OPEC ARCHITECTURE
JEDDAH GCC SUMMIT — UAE SENT FM NOT PRESIDENT (DIPLOMATIC SNUB); SAUDI INFORMATION POSTURE DEFENSIVE 3 DAYS RUNNING; UAE

recent_signals.headline: UAE LEAVES OPEC AND OPEC+ EFFECTIVE MAY 1 — BIGGEST STRUCTURAL OPEC DEVELOPMENT IN A DECADE; BESSENT DOLLAR-SWAP-LINE NE

historical_summaries.summary: 1 signals. Iran struck US Consulate in Dubai, Dubai International Airport, and Fairmont The Palm hotel in initial retaliation wave

5 signals. ADNOC Ruwais refinery (UAE's largest, 922K bpd capacity) shut down after Iranian drone strike caused fire | Iran struck Shah gas field (set ablaze) and Fujairah port (key oil hub used as Hormuz bypass) | IRGC threatened US university campuses in Gulf on March 29, specifically naming NYU Abu Dhabi

15 signals. UAE described as 'perhaps the most hawkish' among Gulf allies, pushing for ground invasion of Iran (WaPo national security desk) | Al-Salmi VLCC (Kuwait-flagged, 2M bbl Saudi/Kuwaiti oil for China) struck at Dubai port anchorage March 31 — most significant single-vessel | CONFIRMED: Borouge petrochemical plant ($60B, world's largest single-site polyolefin plant) SHUT DOWN operations at Ruwais Industrial Comple
